criteria.reason: The live callback path now accepts only auth_requests state, retained telegram_sessions.oauth_state is cleared, and live user-scoped EVE ownership resolves from user_id instead of falling back to chat_id when a real user exists.
checks: npm test -- tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/telegram-request-guard.test.ts tests/unit/frontend-handoff.test.ts tests/unit/prompts.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/user-profile.test.ts

criteria.reason: Telegram ingress now rejects overlapping requests in a chat, enforces a recent-rate window per actor, and fails closed once the global active-request ceiling is reached. The guard is wired into message handling and covered by focused regression tests.
checks: npm test -- tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/telegram-request-guard.test.ts tests/unit/frontend-handoff.test.ts tests/unit/prompts.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/user-profile.test.ts

criteria.reason: Bot-to-web handoff now keeps the bearer token in the URL fragment only, the browser clears it locally, and the server-side session is created through POST /auth/tg-handoff/exchange rather than a query-bearing GET flow.
checks: npm test -- tests/unit/auth-routes.test.ts tests/unit/telegram-request-guard.test.ts tests/unit/frontend-handoff.test.ts tests/unit/prompts.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/user-profile.test.ts

criteria.reason: USER.md still contains full gameplay context, but prompt assembly now frames that content as untrusted data and quotes it line by line so the model receives data rather than instructions.
